<Heading
  as={"h1"}
  className={"openapi__heading"}
  children={"Execute A Manual Trigger"}
>
</Heading>

<MethodEndpoint
  method={"post"}
  path={"/automations/manual-triggers/execute"}
  context={"endpoint"}
>
  
</MethodEndpoint>



Execute a manual trigger within an automation to initiate subsequent conditions and actions.

:::tip
A manual trigger is a trigger that users can add to automation workflows and activate through user operation. It is suitable for scenarios where automation requires manual intervention to start, or for instant activation during testing and debugging.

How to add a manual trigger depends on how you create your Flow automation:
1. If created on the Aqara Studio Web page, refer to: [Automation - Flow - Trigger - Manual Trigger](https://docs.aqara.com/docs/aqara-studio/flow/trigger#manual-trigger)
2. If created by API, add a manual trigger in your JSON script. See: [JSON Automation Script Definition - Starter - Manual](https://docs.aqara.com/docs/aqara-studio/api-references/json-automation-script#manual)
:::

  id={"request"}
  as={"h2"}
  className={"openapi-tabs__heading"}
  children={"Request"}
>
</Heading>

  parameters={[]}
>
  
</ParamsDetails>

  title={"Body"}
  body={{"required":true,"content":{"application/json":{"schema":{"type":"object","required":["automationId","triggerNodeId"],"properties":{"automationId":{"type":"string","description":"Automation ID."},"triggerNodeId":{"type":"string","description":"ID of the manual trigger to be executed."}},"title":"ExecuteAutomationManualTriggerRequest"}}}}}
>
  
</RequestSchema>
